title: Feature/cap estimate gas to 7825

body:
Before Osaka it was enough to check transactions when it was getting into the pool. On new fork rules were not invalidated. Now we have 3 new things that potentially invalidate transaction on new head(which can be new fork):
- tx gas limit (30MGas, with no cap before)
- proof version (v0 but with fork we expect v1, we agreed to not transform proofs as it is too expensive and risky)
- max blob count (can become 6 after was set to 9)

So we need to remove transactions from tx pool in rare case of Osaka fork, we do the checks in UpdateGasBottleneck
With new head such transactions will be removed, but when we are asked to produce a block, they can still be there
So we add additional filtering for transactions when they are being picked by block producer

3 checks in total

## Changes

- Clean up tx pool from such txs on fork activation
- Filter out them in block production


## Types of changes

#### What types of changes does your code introduce?

body:
Closes #4584 
Closes #4586 
Closes #4906
Closes #4907 
Closes #4956  
Closes #5444

## Changes

- * [EOF Spec](https://github.com/ipsilon/eof/blob/main/spec/eof.md)
- * EOF EIPs listed as part of [EIP-7692](https://eips.ethereum.org/EIPS/eip-7692.md), namely: 
    * [EIP-663](https://eips.ethereum.org/EIPS/eip-663.md): SWAPN, DUPN and EXCHANGE instructions
    * [EIP-3540](https://eips.ethereum.org/EIPS/eip-3540.md): EOF - EVM Object Format v1
    * [EIP-3670](https://eips.ethereum.org/EIPS/eip-3670.md): EOF - Code Validation
    * [EIP-4200](https://eips.ethereum.org/EIPS/eip-4200.md): EOF - Static relative jumps
    * [EIP-4750](https://eips.ethereum.org/EIPS/eip-4750.md): EOF - Functions
    * [EIP-5450](https://eips.ethereum.org/EIPS/eip-5450.md): EOF - Stack Validation
    * [EIP-6206](https://eips.ethereum.org/EIPS/eip-6206.md): EOF - JUMPF and non-returning functions
    * [EIP-7069](https://eips.ethereum.org/EIPS/eip-7069.md): Revamped CALL instructions
    * [EIP-7480](https://eips.ethereum.org/EIPS/eip-7480.md): EOF - Data section access instructions
    * [EIP-7620](https://eips.ethereum.org/EIPS/eip-7620.md): EOF Contract Creation
    * [EIP-7698](https://eips.ethereum.org/EIPS/eip-7698.md): EOF - Creation transaction
    * [EIP-7756](https://eips.ethereum.org/EIPS/eip-7756.md): EOF/EVM Trace Specification 
- Refactor Evm to make it less unwieldy to opcode changes & optimizations

## Types of changes

#### What types of changes does your code introduce?

- [x] New feature (a non-breaking change that adds functionality)

body:
## Changes

Engine API [(spec)](https://github.com/ethereum/execution-apis/pull/630/files):
- added `engine_getPayloadV5` and `BlobsBundleV2`
- added `engine_getBlobsV2` and `BlobAndProofV2`

EIP-7594 [(spec)](https://github.com/ethereum/EIPs/pull/9378/files):
- added `ProofVersion` to network wrapper
- added support for version 0x1 of network wrapper (cell proofs)

Other:
- bumped ckzg package from `2.0.1.1258` to `2.1.0.1342`
- added tests for storage of blob transactions with cell proofs

TODO:

- [x] p2p logic (send wrapper 0x00 only before fork and 0x01 only after fork)
- [ ] p2p perf improvement (after fork skip requesting blob txs with size lower than min size of blob tx with cell proofs as it is 100% old format which will be rejected anyway)
- [x] (optional) add config `proofsTranslationEnabled` - if `true`, after the fork accept txs from RPC endpoint with network wrapped in old format and translate to the new one (so calculate cell proofs)
- [ ] benchmark, improve proof verification (and probably proof generation) performance 

## Types of changes

To activate peerdas in chainspec the following changes are required:

```json
{
  ...
  "params": {
    ...
    "blobSchedule": [
      ...
      {
        "timestamp": "osaka timestamp"
        "target": 48,
        "max": 72,
        "baseFeeUpdateFraction": 40061728
      }
    ],
   ...
    "eip7594TransitionTimestamp": "0x..."
  },
  ...
}
```

Nethermind client docker image: `nethermindeth/nethermind:peerdas-c3d12c7`
Send blobs tool docker image: `nethermindeth/send-blobs:latest` (pass `--fork Osaka` flag to send blobs with proof type V2)

#### Requires explanation in Release Notes

- [x] Yes
- [ ] No

PeerDAS support for Fusaka
title: EIP-7594: PeerDAS

body:
Resolves #8847


## Changes


1. Update the [EIP-170](./eip-170.md) contract code size limit of 24KB (`0x6000` bytes) to 256KB (`0x40000` bytes).
2. Change the gas schedule for opcodes which load code. Specifically, the opcodes `CALL`, `STATICCALL`, `DELEGATECALL`, `CALLCODE` and `EXTCODECOPY` are modified so that `largeContractCost = ceil32(excess_contract_size) * GAS_INIT_CODE_WORD_COST // 32` gas is added to the access cost if the code is cold, where `excess_contract_size = max(0, contract_size - 0x6000)`, and `GAS_INIT_CODE_WORD_COST = 2`. (Cf. initcode metering: [EELS](https://github.com/ethereum/execution-specs/blob/1a587803e3e698407d204888b02342393f8b4fe5/src/ethereum/cancun/vm/gas.py#L269)). This introduces a new warm state for contract code - warm if the code has been loaded, cold if not.

| Contract                | Gas changes (only opcodes that load code)                          | How?                                                                                                                       |
| ----------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------ | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| Cold account and code   | Add `largeContractCost` to `COLD_ACCOUNT_ACCESS_COST=2600`         | Contract not in access list nor accessed prior in the txn                                                           |
| Warm account, cold code | Add `largeContractCost` to `WARM_STORAGE_READ_COST=100`         | Already accessed balance, storage, or included in access list ([EIP-2930](./eip-2930.md))                                               |
| Warm account and code   | No change to existing gas schedule. `WARM_STORAGE_READ_COST=100` | Contract created with `CREATE`/`CREATE2`, or `CALL`, `STATICCALL`, `DELEGATECALL`, `CALLCODE` or `EXTCODECOPY` made on the contract, previously in the txn (opcodes that load contract code) |

`COLD_ACCOUNT_ACCESS_COST` and `WARM_STORAGE_READ_COST` are defined in [EIP-2929](./eip-2929.md#parameters).

3. Update the [EIP-3860](./eip-3860.md) contract initcode size limit of 48KB (`0xc000` bytes) to 512KB (`0x80000` bytes).

## Types of changes

#### What types of changes does your code introduce?

- [x] New feature (a non-breaking change that adds functionality)

## Testing

#### Requires testing

- [x] Yes

#### If yes, did you write tests?

- [ ] Yes
- [ ] No
title: Implement Eip7907: Meter Contract Code Size And Increase Limit
